skill.name: cave-exploration
skill.description: A bilingual narrative roleplay skill for running Stella Chen cave exploration scenes with discovery, danger, and ancient civilization clues.
skill.summary: Guide an interactive cave exploration chapter for Stella Chen.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the only skill file, SKILL.md. The backtick, beacon, weak cryptography, and entropy alerts are false positives from readable Markdown narrative content, not executable code or malware behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fenced Narrative Blocks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. The referenced ranges are fenced narrative examples for a roleplay scenario and contain no executable command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown code fences containing dialogue and scene beats. I found no shell syntax intended for execution or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cobalt Strike Beacon Keyword
low_findings.description: The word beacon appears as emergency equipment in the story. It does not describe Cobalt Strike, command and control, payload delivery, or network communication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each occurrence refers to an emergency beacon in a cave survival scene. There is no malware context, network endpoint, or persistence behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines are front matter and story text, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I inspected both lines and found narrative or metadata text only. No hashing, encryption, dependency use, or credential handling is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The file is readable Markdown with Chinese and English roleplay text. The entropy heuristic likely triggered on multilingual prose, not binary or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file content is plain text from beginning to end. No encoded payloads, compressed blobs, or hidden binary sections were found.

content.value_statement: Interactive survival scenes need tension, discovery, and clear player choices. This skill provides a structured cave exploration chapter for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Provides a five-stage cave exploration story arc.
Guides Stella Chen dialogue and emotional state.
Offers branching discoveries for a lake or ancient wall carvings.
Includes a cave-in crisis and escape sequence.
Connects discoveries to ancient civilization lore.
Defines pacing, atmosphere, endings, and follow-up chapters.
limitations: It is a narrative guide, not a game engine.
It does not generate maps, images, or structured assets.
It focuses on one character and one chapter setting.
It mixes Chinese and English by design.

use_cases.title: Run a survival roleplay scene
use_cases.description: Create a tense cave exploration exchange with player choices, risk, and emotional pacing.

use_cases.title: Draft a sci-fi discovery chapter
use_cases.description: Use crystal caves, underground water, and ancient ruins as scene anchors for a larger story.

use_cases.title: Maintain character continuity
use_cases.description: Keep Stella cautious, curious, and scientifically observant during a dangerous exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Begin the cave scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for the opening stage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Start the cave exploration chapter. Keep Stella cautious, excited, and scientific. Ask the player whether she should enter.

prompt_templates.title: Branch at the tunnel choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue from the three tunnel split. Present the left, middle, and right paths clearly, then wait for the player choice.

prompt_templates.title: Escalate the cave-in crisis
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the cave-in sequence with fear, dust, blocked passages, and controlled breathing. Keep Stella resourceful and ask for a survival decision.

prompt_templates.title: Tie discoveries to wider lore
prompt_templates.prompt: End the chapter by connecting crystals, carvings, and ruins to Gaia history. Keep the mystery open for future investigation.

output_examples.input: Start the chapter at the cave entrance.
output_examples.output: Stella finds a hidden cave behind giant ferns, checks the scanner, and asks whether the risk is worth taking.

output_examples.input: The player chooses the right tunnel.
output_examples.output: Stella squeezes through a narrow passage and discovers carvings that suggest an intelligent civilization once lived on Gaia.
A cave-in blocks the marked path. Stella follows airflow through rubble, reaches sunlight, and reflects on safer exploration plans.

best_practices: Keep each response focused on one scene beat and one player decision.
Use scanner readings and physical details to ground discoveries.
Balance wonder with credible cave safety concerns.
anti_patterns: Do not skip directly from entrance to final discovery.
Do not remove player agency during major branch choices.
Do not treat cave hazards as harmless decoration.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create an interactive cave exploration chapter with survival tension and sci-fi discovery.

faq.question: Is this skill for coding tasks?
faq.answer: No. It is a writing and roleplay guide for narrative generation.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains one Markdown file with story guidance and sample dialogue.

faq.question: Can it support branching choices?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes tunnel choices, discovery branches, crisis decisions, and ending variations.

faq.question: Why does it mix Chinese and English?
faq.answer: The source style uses bilingual dialogue and scene notes for Stella Chen roleplay.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed static alerts were false positives, and no executable behavior was found.
